title: Splunk Web UI Errors (_internal)
spl:
index=_internal sourcetype IN ("splunk_web_access","splunkd_ui","splunkd") uri_path="*"
| search status>=500 OR match(_raw,"(?i)(error|exception|timeout)")
| stats count by status, uri_path, clientip
| sort -count
description: Web UI 5xx/timeout errors block analysts during incidents. Tracking errors isolates SHC vs load balancer vs app issues.
value: Web UI 5xx/timeout errors block analysts during incidents. Tracking errors isolates SHC vs load balancer vs app issues.
grandmaExplanation: Web UI 5xx/timeout errors block analysts during incidents ‚Äî so you always trust the numbers you are looking at.
implementation: Ensure access logs include HTTP status. Alert on 5xx rate above baseline. Correlate with KV Store and SHC captain during UI-wide failures.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Monitoring Console.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=_internal` `sourcetype=splunk_web_access` / `splunkd_ui` / `splunkd` Web UI.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Ensure access logs include HTTP status. Alert on 5xx rate above baseline. Correlate with KV Store and SHC captain during UI-wide failures.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=_internal sourcetype IN ("splunk_web_access","splunkd_ui","splunkd") uri_path="*"
| search status>=500 OR match(_raw,"(?i)(error|exception|timeout)")
| stats count by status, uri_path, clientip
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Splunk Web UI Errors (_internal)** ‚Äî Web UI 5xx/timeout errors block analysts during incidents. Tracking errors isolates SHC vs load balancer vs app issues.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=_internal` `sourcetype=splunk_web_access` / `splunkd_ui` / `splunkd` Web UI. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Monitoring Console. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: _internal.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=_internal. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by status, uri_path, clientip** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (5xx rate), Table (top failing URIs).
visualization: Line chart (5xx rate), Table (top failing URIs).
